title: TenSura: Reincarnated Overhaul
description: TenSura:Reincarnated Overhaul aims to add every canon aspect of Tensura to the original mod.
body:
Welcome to TenSura:Overhaul!

TenSura:Overhaul is a mod that aims to add EVERY canon thing to Tensura:Reincarnated, and ONLY canon things, even things other mods have implemented. This includes the anime, manga, light novel, video games, and spin-offs. For more info, see below.

We hope to add many Races, Skills, Spells, Armors, Weapons, and even new Mechanics from Tensura!! We are here to do our best and bring you the best we can offer, and we hope you'll support and enjoy our Addon every step of the way!


We are looking for everyone possible to help us out and join! If you want to be part of a small community that will eventually be HUGE, join up! And if you’re good at building, coding, server moderation, or designing, we would love to have you! 


So far, TenSura:Overhaul adds 5 unique skills: Avenger, Manipulator, Tempter, Analyst, and Imitator. And more in the making!


Join the Discord for answers to any questions, comments, or concerns, and to see the wiki.
[https://discord.gg/MyvtfK4Qse](https://discord.gg/MyvtfK4Qse)

 


There is a system that supports having skills of the same name called the Overhaul Override System. There's a gamemode (called Overhaul Override System), and each number you can put in means a different thing:
Default (mode 4, blacklist-only): override everything except blacklisted mods. (Example: If another mod isn’t blacklisted and they have Tempter, troverhaul:tempter overrides theirs)
Mode 0 (nothing): OOS not active.
Mode 1 (all): always override. All other skills are overridden.
Mode 2 (none): never override. Other skills override troverhaul's.
Mode 3 (whitelist-only): only override if the other mod’s name is whitelisted.

There are also commands for adding or removing skills from being overridden.

/troverhaul overrides specific add <skillId> — adds a specific skill to the override list.
/troverhaul overrides specific remove <skillId> — removes it.
/troverhaul overrides specific list — lists all explicit overrides.

What does override mean? It means that either a skill from TenSura:Overhaul replaces a skill of the same name from another addon, or vice versa, in the skill reincarnation list. Normally, both skills would be on the reincarnation list, but this system gets rid of one or the other (unless it's Mode 0).

 


Plus: many thanks to our entire team, especially Dumpy, Sugma, Alyssa, Blast, and Diego! You are all very appreciated.
